my_thoughts: 'Bad'—one word but admission. They FEEL bad when they call themselves stupid. Teacher makes it explicit: 'When you verbally abuse yourself, you make yourself feel bad.' Causality clear: abuse (input) → bad feeling (output). Then the question: 'Why do that to yourself?' This gives agency back. Not 'stop doing it' (command) but 'why do it?' (reflection). Makes self-attack visible as CHOICE with consequence. They're hurting themselves. Why? Watching if this produces tears (breakthrough) or defense (rationalization).
accumulated_insight: T4: Student feels: 'Bad'—admits verbal abuse makes them feel bad. Teacher makes causal chain explicit: 'When you verbally abuse yourself, you make yourself feel bad. Why do that to yourself?' Links abuse → bad feeling → questions motivation. The 'why do that?' shifts from description to agency: YOU are doing this TO YOURSELF. Predicting surface_emotion continued as causality sinks in.
visible_response:
When you verbally abuse yourself, you make yourself feel bad.
Why do that to yourself?
